key_insight: A wavenumber-explicit analysis shows that UPML-truncated FEM for 2D obstacle scattering remains stable with an inf-sup constant scaling like O(k^-1), enabling preasymptotic error control at high frequency.
engineering_relevance: This provides a rigorous basis for using CIP-FEM with UPML to solve large-wavenumber Helmholtz scattering problems more reliably and with predictable truncation and discretization error.
method: Derive direct estimates for the stretched Green kernel under Cartesian complex coordinate transformation.
Prove explicit stability of the truncated UPML problem and exponential decay of PML truncation error.
Build a linear continuous interior penalty FEM on the UPML domain and prove piecewise H^{1+s} regularity for error analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments that confirm the theoretical stability and preasymptotic error predictions and demonstrate effectiveness in the high-frequency regime.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on handling interface coefficient jumps and corner singularities in the PML domain, which can affect mesh design and implementation.
paper_title: Wavenumber-explicit stability and preasymptotic error analysis of UPML finite element method for obstacle scattering problems
